question_text: Two identical bulbs are connected in a parallel circuit to a single battery. How does the brightness of each bulb compare to when a single bulb is connected to the same battery?
correct_answer: Each bulb is equally bright (or slightly less bright)
explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch receives the full voltage from the battery. Both bulbs get the same voltage as a single bulb would, so they shine with similar brightness. They may be very slightly dimmer because the battery has to supply current to both bulbs at once.
